title: Karaage Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken

seo.title: Crispy Karaage Recipe: Authentic Japanese Fried Chicken
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best crispy Karaage (Japanese Fried Chicken) at home! This authentic recipe delivers juicy, flavorful chicken with an irresistible crunch.
seo.h1: Karaage: Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What is Karaage Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Plate of crispy Karaage Japanese fried chicken with lemon wedges and dipping sauce
seo.imageCaption: Golden brown and perfectly crispy Karaage, ready to be enjoyed!

description: Karaage is a beloved Japanese fried chicken, renowned for its incredibly crispy exterior and wonderfully juicy, flavorful meat. Marinated in a savory blend of soy sauce, ginger, and garlic, then coated in a light starch, each bite offers a delightful crunch followed by tender, umami-rich chicken that is simply irresistible.

ingredients.notes: boneless, skin-on, cut into 3-4 cm pieces
for deep frying (vegetable, canola, or peanut oil)

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Cut boneless, skin-on chicken thighs into 3-4 cm bite-sized pieces. Pat them dry thoroughly with paper towels to ensure the marinade adheres well and the chicken crisps up nicely.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the soy sauce, sake, mirin, grated ginger, grated garlic, and sesame oil (if using). Add the chicken pieces and mix well to coat. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours, allowing the flavors to penetrate the chicken.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken
instructions.text: After marinating, remove the chicken from the refrigerator and drain any excess liquid. In a separate bowl, whisk the egg. Dip each marinated chicken piece into the beaten egg, then dredge it thoroughly in the potato starch, ensuring an even coating on all sides.

instructions.title: First Fry
instructions.text: Heat the oil in a deep pot or Dutch oven to 160C (320F). Fry the chicken in batches, being careful not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 2-3 minutes per batch, until the chicken is lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispiness
instructions.text: Once all the chicken has been fried once, increase the oil temperature to 180C (350F). Return the chicken to the hot oil in batches and fry for an additional 1-2 minutes, until deeply golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique ensures maximum crunch.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Remove the crispy Karaage from the oil and drain on a wire rack. Serve piping hot with fresh lemon wedges for a bright finish and a side of Japanese mayonnaise for dipping. Enjoy this classic Japanese comfort food!

tips: For truly authentic Karaage, always use boneless, skin-on chicken thighs. The fat from the skin renders beautifully, keeping the chicken juicy and adding rich flavor.
The secret to ultimate crispiness is the double-frying method. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the second fry at a higher temperature creates that irresistible golden, crunchy crust.
Serve Karaage immediately after the second fry for the best texture. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens the flavor, and Japanese Kewpie mayonnaise makes an excellent dipping sauce.
article: Karaage Crispy Japanese Fried Chicken is a beloved Japanese Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
